question: The smallest section of a rod which can be cut 36cm in length is
A sum of money was invested at 8% per annum simple interest. If after 4 years, the   money became
Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose interior angle is twice the exterior angle.
solution:
Let the ext. angle = x
Thus int. angle = 2x
But sum of int + ext = 180 (angle of a straight line).
2x + x = 180
3x = 180
x = 180/3 = 60

Each ext angle = 360/n
=> 60 = 360/n
n = 360/60 = 6

question: In the diagram above calculate the value of x
solution:
p + 40° = 100° (exterior ∠ = sum of two interior opp ∠s)
p = 100° - 40°
P = 60°
But q + p = 180° (∠s on a straight line)
q + 60°
q = 180° - 60°
q = 120°
x = q (corresponding ∠)
∴x = 120°

question: Find the gradient of the line passing through the points P(1, 1) and Q(2, 5).
solution:
Let (x1, y1) = (1, 1) and (x2, y2)= (2, 5)

then gradient m of PQ¯ is

m = y<sub>2</sub>−y<sub>1</sub>/x2−x1 = 5−1/2−1

= 4/1

= 4

question: In triangle PQR, q = 8 cm, r = 6 cm and cos P = 1/12 Calculate the value of
solution:
Using the cosine rule, we have

p<sup>2</sup> = q<sup>2</sup> + r<sup>2</sup> − 2qrcosP
p<sup>2</sup> = 8<sup>2</sup> + 6<sup>2</sup> − 2(8)(6)(1/12)
= 64 + 36 − 8
p<sup>2</sup> = 92∴ p = √9cm

question: Find the range of the following set of numbers 0.4, −0.4, 0.3, 0.47, −0.53, 0.2 and −0.2
solution:
0.4, −0.4, 0.3, 0.47, −0.53, 0.2, −0.2

Range is the difference between the highest and lowest value

i.e Highest − Lowest

− 0.53, −0.4, −0.2, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.47

0.47 is the highest

− 0.53 is the lowest

∴ = 0.47 − (− 0.53)

∴0.47 + 0.53

= 1.0

question: What is the loci of a distance 4cm from a given point P?
solution: Locus is the path traced at by a point which moves in accordance with a certain law. It is also the set of all possible position occupied by an object The path traced from all possible location of 4cm from a given point P form a circle of radius 4cm with centre P.

question: Find the value of x if √2/(x+√2)= 1/√(x−2)
A man sells his new brand car for N420,000 at a gain of 15%. What did it cost him?
solution:
Cost Price:

Selling price = 100 : (100 + 15)

= 100 : 115

Cost Price

= 100 ÷ 115 of selling price (i.e N420,000)

= 100 ÷ 115 × N420,000

= N365,217.4

∴ Cost Price = N365, 217
